paper_title: Al-in-Hornblende Geobarometry and Thermobarometry in Kaikomagatake Pluton

methods.description: Process for selecting and preparing samples from the Kaikomagatake pluton for Al-in-hornblende geobarometry.

steps.description: Eight samples of the Kaikomagatake pluton collected from the internal domain were selected for Al-in-hornblende geobarometry.
steps.searchable_summary: select samples from Kaikomagatake pluton for geobarometry

methods.description: Application of Al-in-hornblende geobarometers to estimate pressure conditions of granitoid magmas.

steps.description: We applied two Al-in hornblende geobarometers; (1) the recently proposed geobarometer for relatively shallow granitoid magmas (Mutch et al., 2016) and (2) conventionally applied geobarometer that incorporates the effects of temperature (Anderson and Smith, 1995).
steps.searchable_summary: apply Al-in-hornblende geobarometers for pressure estimation

methods.description: Estimation of hornblende crystallization temperatures using Ti-in-amphibole thermometer.

steps.description: We estimated the temperatures of hornblende crystallization using Ti-in-amphibole thermometer applicable to calcic amphiboles crystallizing in Ti-saturated calc-alkaline magma.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate crystallization temperatures using Ti-in-amphibole thermometer

methods.description: Use of WinAmptb program for thermobarometry calculations.

steps.description: We used the WinAmptb program (Yavuz and Döner, 2017) for the thermobarometry calculations.

methods.description: Analysis of hornblende samples using scanning electron microscopy with energy dispersive spectroscopy (SEM-EDS).

steps.description: Hornblende analyses were carried out using scanning electron microscopy with energy dispersive spectroscopy (SEM-EDS; JEOL JSM-6510LV and Oxford Instruments X-Max 50) at the Department of Earth Sciences, Graduate School of Science and Engineering, Ehime University, Japan.
The operating conditions were 15 kV accelerating voltage and 0.8 nA beam current. A counting time of 50 seconds was used for quantitative analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: set SEM-EDS operating conditions for analysis

steps.description: We used standard materials of SiO2 for Si, TiO2 for Ti, Al2O3 for Al, Fe2SiO4 for Fe, MnTiO3 for Mn, Mg2SiO4 for Mg, CaSiO3 for Ca, NaAlSi2O6 for Na, and KAlSi3O8 for K. Co standard was also used to optimize the quantification of the analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: use standard materials for SEM-EDS quantification
